skill.name: pptx
skill.description: Create, edit, analyze, validate, and preview PowerPoint presentations using HTML-to-PPTX and Office Open XML workflows.
skill.summary: Build and modify PowerPoint decks with HTML conversion, template workflows, OOXML editing, validation, and thumbnails.
skill.author: Azeem-2

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many patterns across documentation, OOXML schema files, and helper scripts. Most weak cryptography, C2, hardcoded URL, and markdown backtick findings are false positives from schema vocabulary, examples, or legitimate local tooling. The confirmed concern is unsafe archive extraction of user-supplied Office files, plus local converter execution that should require sandboxing before marketplace publication.

high_findings.title: Unsafe archive extraction of Office files
high_findings.description: The unpack and validation helpers extract user-supplied Office archives with extractall. A crafted PPTX, DOCX, or related archive can contain traversal paths that write outside the intended directory unless members are validated first.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The static finding is confirmed by direct use of archive extractall on Office files supplied to the workflow. This is a known Zip Slip class risk and the code shown does not validate member paths before extraction.

medium_findings.title: External document converters run on user-provided files
medium_findings.description: Helper scripts invoke local tools such as soffice, pdftoppm, and git through subprocess.run. The commands use argument arrays rather than shell interpolation, but they still process untrusted document content with complex local parsers.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command execution is real and semantically part of the skill workflow. Command injection evidence is low because calls use argv lists, but sandboxing is still appropriate for untrusted Office inputs.

medium_findings.title: Skill instructions request privileged package installation
medium_findings.description: The dependency section includes sudo apt-get commands for LibreOffice and Poppler. This is installation guidance rather than hidden execution, but marketplace users should not run privileged commands from a community skill without review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are documented setup commands, not automatic escalation. The risk is operational because the skill explicitly tells users or agents how to install system packages with sudo.

low_findings.title: Environment variable use limited to temporary directory selection
low_findings.description: The JavaScript converter reads TMPDIR to choose a browser temporary directory. I did not find evidence that secrets are read or transmitted from environment variables.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scanner correctly found environment access, but reviewed context shows TMPDIR usage. No evidence found for credential harvesting or network exfiltration from environment values.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs are mostly schema namespaces and documentation links
low_findings.description: The URL findings largely appear in OOXML namespace constants, XML schema files, and examples. I did not find evidence that these URLs are fetched for data exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network patterns are real strings, but semantic review shows namespace and license URLs. No evidence found of outbound requests to those locations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Archive extractall without path validation
dangerous_patterns.description: Multiple scripts call extractall while handling Office files, which are ZIP archives. This pattern should be replaced with safe extraction that rejects absolute paths and parent-directory traversal.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is directly present at the cited locations and applies to user-controlled document archives. The mitigation is well defined and should be required before publication.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local command execution for conversion workflows
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill relies on local command execution for document validation and thumbnail generation. These commands are legitimate for the domain but expand the attack surface when processing untrusted files.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The static command findings are confirmed, but reviewed calls use fixed executable names and argument arrays. This supports a medium operational risk rather than confirmed malicious execution.

content.value_statement: Building polished PowerPoint files by hand can be slow and error prone. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through PPTX creation, template editing, validation, and visual review.
actual_capabilities: Creates new PowerPoint decks from HTML slide layouts using the included html2pptx JavaScript workflow.
Edits existing PPTX files by unpacking Office Open XML, changing XML, validating changes, and repacking.
Analyzes template decks through text extraction, thumbnail grids, slide inventories, and placeholder mapping.
Rearranges template slides with duplicate, delete, and reorder workflows based on zero-indexed slide mappings.
Replaces slide text while preserving paragraph properties such as bullets, alignment, font size, and color.
Generates thumbnail grids for visual inspection of layout, contrast, clipping, and slide consistency.
limitations: Requires local dependencies such as Python packages, Node packages, LibreOffice, Poppler, and browser automation.
Works best when the user provides source content, brand direction, or a template deck.
Complex PowerPoint features can require direct OOXML edits and manual validation.
Untrusted PPTX files should be handled in a sandbox because helper scripts unpack archives and run converters.

use_cases.title: Build a new presentation
use_cases.description: Create a structured slide deck from an outline, with styled HTML slides converted into a PPTX file.

use_cases.title: Adapt an existing template
use_cases.description: Analyze a branded template, select suitable layouts, replace placeholder text, and export a polished deck.

use_cases.title: Audit and repair a deck
use_cases.description: Inspect slide text, notes, comments, XML structure, and thumbnails to find content or formatting issues.

prompt_templates.title: Create a simple deck
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new presentation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a five-slide PowerPoint presentation about [topic] for [audience]. Use a clear structure, readable typography, and a professional visual style.

prompt_templates.title: Edit an existing deck
prompt_templates.scenario: Update content in a provided PPTX file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this PPTX file, summarize its structure, then update the slides with the new content below while preserving the existing design.

prompt_templates.title: Use a branded template
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a deck from a template file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this template deck, create an inventory of useful slide layouts, then build a new presentation using the content outline below.

prompt_templates.title: Validate a generated deck
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate thumbnails for this PPTX, inspect every slide for clipping, overlap, contrast, and layout consistency, then fix any issues and validate the file.

output_examples.input: Create a board update deck about quarterly product progress.
output_examples.output: A PowerPoint file with an executive summary, milestone timeline, metrics slide, risks slide, and next steps slide.

output_examples.input: Update this sales template with new messaging and customer proof points.
output_examples.output: A revised PPTX that keeps the original template design while replacing placeholder text with tailored sales content.

output_examples.input: Check this presentation for layout problems before delivery.
output_examples.output: A visual review summary with identified slide issues and an updated deck after correcting text cutoff and spacing problems.

best_practices: Provide the target audience, slide count, source content, and brand requirements before asking for a deck.
Use thumbnail review after generation to catch clipping, overlap, low contrast, and incorrect slide order.
Sandbox untrusted Office files before unpacking or converting them with local helper scripts.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for complex template edits without providing the actual template file.
Do not skip validation after direct OOXML edits because small XML errors can corrupt the deck.
Do not process unknown PPTX files on a sensitive machine without isolation.

faq.question: Can this skill create a PowerPoint file from scratch?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses an HTML-to-PPTX workflow and PptxGenJS to build new slides with controlled layout.

faq.question: Can it edit an existing presentation?
faq.answer: Yes. It can unpack PPTX files, edit Office Open XML, validate changes, and repack the presentation.

faq.question: Can it use my company template?
faq.answer: Yes. It can inventory template slides, choose suitable layouts, and replace text while preserving design structure.

faq.question: Does it require extra software?
faq.answer: Yes. Some workflows require Python libraries, Node packages, LibreOffice, Poppler, and browser automation.

faq.question: Can it check visual quality?
faq.answer: Yes. It can generate thumbnail grids and inspect slides for text cutoff, overlap, contrast, and layout issues.

faq.question: Is it safe for untrusted files?
faq.answer: Use caution. Some scripts unpack archives and run document converters, so untrusted files should be processed in a sandbox.
